summary: A young DHH (21 years old) reflects on how impressive thinkers simultaneously inspire and provoke envy. He names several early web writers—Joel Spolsky, Dave Winer, Ev Williams, and the 37signals team—as people whose clear thinking triggers this reaction, alongside a newer blogger called 'the talking moose.' He frames blogging as his own path toward growing wise rather than stubborn, and expresses genuine delight at receiving a small compliment on his URL.
key_points: Inspiration and envy are intertwined responses to encountering clear, clever thinking
DHH's early intellectual influences included Joel Spolsky, Dave Winer, Ev Williams, and 37signals—years before he would join 37signals himself
At 21, DHH was already conscious of the risk of becoming closed-minded with age and saw blogging as a discipline against it
The post reveals DHH's pre-fame aspirations: eager to be noticed and to produce content worthy of the thinkers he admired
money_quotes: Impressive people have a tendency to invoke feelings of inspiration and envy at the same time.
This twosome of feelings usually comes by knocking on my brain whenever I read the products of clear and clever thinking.
My fears of growing old and stubborn almost disappear when I think of the opportunity to grow old and wise.
By keeping this blog I hope I don't bore anyone to death in trying to reach the latter.
I'm going to try hard to also make him care for the content behind it.
key_insight: Before becoming a famous contrarian voice himself, DHH was a 21-year-old aspiring to match the clarity of the web thinkers he admired, already wrestling with the tension between inspiration and envy that drives creative ambition.
